description: Confounder: The model assumes the maximum weight occurs only at t ≈ 0.592 and that other t v

_meta.explanation: The rule 'The min_snr_5 weighting function assigns higher loss weights to smaller timesteps (near data), with maximum weight occurring at t = 1 - 1/√6 ≈ 0.592.' does NOT hold here. The model assumes the maximum weight occurs only at t ≈ 0.592 and that other t values yield strictly lower weights. However, cosine_sample_median() returns t = 1 - cos(π/4) ≈ 0.2929, which is in the region where SNR > 5, so the weight becomes 5/(SNR+1), and due to the clamping, the function is not monotonic near t=0 — creating a local maximum that the model doesn't anticipate.
_meta.curriculum_note: Rule 'The min_snr_5 weighting function assigns higher loss weights to smaller timesteps (near data), with maximum weight occurring at t = 1 - 1/√6 ≈ 0.592.' holds in the obvious case but breaks here. Distractor B is the hard negative — the rule naively applied.
